text:
The U.S. womenГўВҖВҷs national teamГўВҖВҷsOlympic squad is set. New coach Emma Hayes announced her first major tournament roster with the USWNT on Wednesday, revealing the 18 players and four alternates who will be heading to Paris.

#Paris2024ready Г°ВҹВҮВәГ°ВҹВҮВё

The biggest headline from Wednesday's announcement: Alex Morgan will not be competing in her fourth Olympics. The three-time Olympian will miss the Games for the first time since 2008, failing to make HayesГўВҖВҷs roster. The 34-year-old has been working back from an ankle injury this spring and hasnГўВҖВҷt quite found the form many USWNT fans are accustomed to seeing from the San Diego Wave star. The depth of the U.S. forward pool, which features the likes of Mallory Swanson and Trinity Rodman, only made MorganГўВҖВҷs task of making the team harder. With the Olympic roster confined to just 18 spots, Hayes elected to call in players with more positional versatility, as Morgan operates primarily as a true No. 9.

ГўВҖВңIt was a tough decision of course, especially considering AlexГўВҖВҷs history and her record with this team but I felt I wanted to go in another direction and selected other players,ГўВҖВқ said Hayes, speaking with media members after WednesdayГўВҖВҷs roster reveal. ГўВҖВңHaving a roster that could adapt is essential. We have a tight turnaround between games. So of course, having players on the roster that could play more than one position mattered with squad depth.ГўВҖВқ

The USWNT coach also opted for youth, naming nine first-time Olympians to her roster, including defender Jenna Nighswonger, midfielder Sam Coffey and forward Jaedyn Shaw. Just 10 players from the U.S.ГўВҖВҷs 2023 World Cup campaign will be on the plane to Paris, and only eight holdovers remain from the ГўВҖВҷ20 Olympics.

ГўВҖВңOne of the biggest things for me ever since I got the job was I want to provide opportunities for less experienced players. There was such a big gap from the top experienced and the rest, and we really have to bring that up,ГўВҖВқ said Hayes. ГўВҖВңThe volume of players that havenГўВҖВҷt played more than 30 caps was really, really noticeable for me. So the last eight months have been about giving experiences to bridge that gap because there is a correlation between caps played and success in international tournaments.ГўВҖВқ

Crystal Dunn is among the few veterans on the team, but she will be filling a much different role at the Paris Games than she has in previous World Cups and Olympics. Moving up the pitch and into the attack, Dunn adds variability to the front line as a player who has spent most of her USWNT career playing at outside back. ItГўВҖВҷs a testament to Dunn that she has consistently been named to major tournament rosters in a position that is not her preferred place on the pitch (nor her best). The 2024 Games provides the 31-year-old with a massive stage to showcase the attacking prowess sheГўВҖВҷs exhibited at the club level for nearly a decade.

If there were any other surprises on the roster, it would be the inclusion of midfielder Korbin Albert, who many considered a bubble player heading into the Olympics. SheГўВҖВҷs been receiving consistent call-ups into USWNT camps since late 2023 and has racked up 11 appearances with the squad. Considering HayesГўВҖВҷs emphasis on versatility and flexibility, however, itГўВҖВҷs not a total shock that the PSG star made the roster, as she can play all three midfield positions.

AlbertГўВҖВҷs time with the USWNT hasnГўВҖВҷt been without controversy though, with the 20-year-old coming under fire in March for liking and sharing anti-LGBTQIA+ posts on social media. She laterapologizedfor her social media activity, while then-captains Lindsey Horan and Morganissued a statementon the incident.

ГўВҖВңKorbin making the team is on merit in terms of what she offers for us in the midfield positions,ГўВҖВқ said Hayes. ГўВҖВңI think when it comes to the situation I came into, there is no denying there has been a lot of work that's been going on in the background to work with Korbin.

ГўВҖВңMy experience with her has been ГўВҖВҰ she is a young person whoГўВҖВҷs understood fully the implications of her social media activity.ГўВҖВқ

The clock is already ticking for Hayes, who has a very short window to bring this relatively young and inexperienced team together. Only joining the U.S. in late MayГўВҖВ“after concluding an illustrious career with the WomenГўВҖВҷs Super LeagueГўВҖВҷs ChelseaГўВҖВ”Hayes has just two friendlies under her belt as head coach. The back-to-back multi-goal shutouts against South Korea in June were a promising start, and Hayes will have two more in-game opportunities to cultivate chemistry before taking off for Paris.

The USWNT will hold two send-off friendlies on the East Coast next monthГўВҖВ”the first against Mexico on July 13 and the second against Costa Rica on July 16ГўВҖВ”with Hayes likely to solidify her starting lineup less than a month before the teamГўВҖВҷs first Olympic matchup.

While itГўВҖВҷs clear that HayesГўВҖВҷs ultimate directive is developing the pool of young talent for the next World Cup cycle, the U.S. is still used to taking home silverware. The Paris Games will be an opportunity for Hayes to best the teamГўВҖВҷs bronze-medal performance from the Tokyo Olympics, and right the ship after a disappointing 2023 World Cup.

Clare Brennan is an associate editor for Sports Illustrated focused on womenГўВҖВҷs sports. Before joining SI in October 2022, she worked as an associate editor at Just WomenГўВҖВҷs Sports and as an associate producer for WDET in Detroit. Brennan has a bachelorГўВҖВҷs in international studies from the University of Wisconsin and a masterГўВҖВҷs in art history from Wayne State University.

title: USWNTГўВҖВҷs Paris Olympics Squad Focuses on Youth, Versatility
description: With Alex Morgan not included in Emma HayesГўВҖВҷs roster, the new coach hopes a younger group can win gold this summer.
